items.title: Health Spending Isn’t A Cost, But Core Public Investment — Dr Kelvin Yii
items.summary: Health spending cuts may worsen the health workforce crisis, especially in Sabah and Sarawak. MOH must never be seen as an easy target for savings. Health spending isn’t a cost to minimise, but a core public investment. MOF must reconsider the cuts. The post Health Spending Isn’t A Cost, But Core Public Investment — Dr Kelvin Yii appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Health Spending Cut Will Worsen Patient Outcomes, Strain Health Workers: Doctor Groups
items.summary: FPMPAM says a RM3 billion health spending cut will likely have "real consequences" for both patients (worse outcomes) and health workers (rising burnout and resignations), while MMA says the public health care system is already under "severe strain". The post Health Spending Cut Will Worsen Patient Outcomes, Strain Health Workers: Doctor Groups appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Medicine Prices Not Rising Yet But API Costs To Hit Soon: Importers
items.summary: Drug prices in Malaysia haven’t risen yet, but increases are expected as higher API costs feed through, says MAPS. Heavy reliance on imported API exposes Malaysia’s medicine supply to shocks, though delays haven’t caused nationwide shortages yet. The post Medicine Prices Not Rising Yet But API Costs To Hit Soon: Importers appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Private Health Facilities Face ‘Savage’ Exposure To Drug, Consumable Supply Shocks: Galen Centre
items.summary: Private health care providers in Malaysia face greater risk from global supply shocks for drugs and consumables due to limited stocks and market exposure, while public hospitals are buffered but strained by heavy patient loads, says the Galen Centre. The post Private Health Facilities Face ‘Savage’ Exposure To Drug, Consumable Supply Shocks: Galen Centre appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Cut Perks For Ministers, MPs, Top Civil Servants Before Gutting Public Health Care — Specialist
items.summary: The government should cut allowances and perks for ministers and deputies, MPs and high-ranking civil servants first before slashing MOH's budget by RM3 billion. Disclose exactly what will be cut from health care. If nothing critical is affected, prove it. The post Cut Perks For Ministers, MPs, Top Civil Servants Before Gutting Public Health Care — Specialist appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: RM3b Cut To Health Budget Is A Prescription For Crisis — Dr Musa Mohd Nordin, Prof Emeritus Dr Azizi Omar & Prof Dr Zulkifli Ismail
items.summary: An RM3 billion cut to the health budget will lead to delayed diagnoses, disease progression, and avoidable deaths. The health workforce crisis will deepen and primary/preventive care will suffer first. This is a penny-wise, pound-foolish approach. The post RM3b Cut To Health Budget Is A Prescription For Crisis — Dr Musa Mohd Nordin, Prof Emeritus Dr Azizi Omar & Prof Dr Zulkifli Ismail appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MOH’s Proposed RM3b Budget Cut Risks Worsening Health Care Crisis: Galen Centre
items.summary: Galen Centre for Health and Social Policy CEO Azrul Mohd Khalib warned a RM3.06b MOH budget cut would mean longer waits, shortages, staff burnout, and preventable deaths. He said the cut would not even match the RM4b spent monthly on RON95 and diesel subsidies. The post MOH’s Proposed RM3b Budget Cut Risks Worsening Health Care Crisis: Galen Centre appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Ascott Makes Full Switch To Cage-Free Eggs In Malaysia Amid Cost, Supply Concerns
items.summary: Ascott has fully switched to cage-free eggs across its Malaysian properties, positioning the move as scalable despite industry concerns over cost and supply. Stakeholders say it shows that large-scale adoption is feasible and could support wider industry uptake. The post Ascott Makes Full Switch To Cage-Free Eggs In Malaysia Amid Cost, Supply Concerns appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Report: Treasury Wants RM3b Cut To Health Ministry’s 2026 Budget
items.summary: The Treasury has ordered budget cuts for various ministries this year due to a ballooning subsidy bill, specifying a proposed cut of about RM3 billion (6.6%) to the Health Ministry's RM46.5 billion 2026 budget, leaving MOH with a smaller budget than 2025. The post Report: Treasury Wants RM3b Cut To Health Ministry’s 2026 Budget appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Reintroduce Tobacco GEG Under Act 852 — MCTC
items.summary: Malaysia should follow the UK's example and reintroduce the tobacco generational end game (GEG) under the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 (Act 852) to protect future generations from nicotine addiction. The post Reintroduce Tobacco GEG Under Act 852 — MCTC appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 30 — The government’s recent expenditure adjustment measures will not affect essential publ...

items.title: Penang finances still strong, but some projects delayed amid cost pressures, says chief minister
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 30 — Penang’s financial position is still healthy so there is no need for a supplementary...
